_patch_meta.description: MTI status assignments for 84 mti=None active terms (31 anger-relevant → extracted/extracted_thin, 53 bleed → delete), data error correction (H7110B), 6 Phase 2 flags, and registry status update.

set.status_note: Session B v3.0. Noun form of ka.as provocation family. 22 verses covering provocation of God and human vexation. Companion to H3707 (verb) and H3708B. Includes significant Ecclesiastes vexation-of-wisdom cluster.
Session B v3.0. 14 verses, exclusively of divine anger. Used in Deuteronomy for God's anger at Moses and Israel. Cognate of H0639 (aph) root. Analytically significant as a divine-only anger verb.

operations.description: H2194 za.am be indignant verb — extracted

set.status_note: Session B v3.0. 11 verses. Verbal form of za.am indignation. Psa 7:11 (God feels indignation every day) is key. Boundary case with abomination registry (reg 1) which holds the same root verbal form H2194. Phase 2 cross-registry investigation required (PH2-004-002).
Session B v3.0. 6 verses. Adjective of burning intensity used in cho.ri aph compounds. Both human (1Sa 20:34, Jonathan) and divine (Lam 2:3) anger. Heat-cluster member.
Session B v3.0. 2 verses (2Ki 19:3; Isa 37:3). Noun of contempt/disgrace. Cognate of na.ats (H5006). Thin data; anger-boundary term.
Session B v3.0. 3 verses (Neh 9:18, 26; Eze 35:12). Contempt/reviling directed at God. Thin data; anger-boundary term, companion to H5007A.

Session B v3.0. 40 verses. Spans trembling, agitation, and rage. OT background for Eph 4:26 (Psa 4:4). Holds somatic and psychological registers of intense arousal together. Analytically significant for anger-fear comparison in Session D.
Session B v3.0. 1 verse (Eph 4:26, anchor verse). The anger that must not be allowed to persist overnight. Companion noun to parorgizō verb. Analytically significant as the anger the devil exploits.
Session B v3.0. 1 verse (Tit 1:7). Quick-tempered as a character disqualifier for eldership. Links anger to the Being dimension — dispositional anger as incompatible with pastoral character.

Session B v3.0. 2 verses (Act 15:39; Heb 10:24). The stirring-up mechanism used both negatively (Paul-Barnabas dispute) and positively (stir up to love and good deeds). Analytically interesting ambivalence in a single term. Phase 2 note PH2-004-006.
Session B v3.0. 1 verse (Act 12:20). Herod's anger at Tyre and Sidon. Thumos-root compound. Thin data but lexically informative.

Session B v3.0 — DATA ERROR. H7110B is a homonym (splinter, Joel 1:7) with no anger content. Its verse count of 28 is erroneously shared with H7110A (qe.tseph: wrath). H7110A holds the correct verse data. H7110B should be removed from the anger term inventory.

operations.description: Set delete status for 53 lexical bleed terms with no anger content

record.description: H7307J (ruach: temper) places the governance of anger at the spirit level. Pro 16:32; 17:27; 25:28; 29:11 treat ruling the spirit as the governing capacity for anger. Cross-registry analysis with spirit (reg 184) required before the anger-spirit boundary can be mapped. Session D priority question: is governing anger a spirit operation, and what does that imply for the spirit-soul boundary?

Za.am family (H2194 verbal, H2195 nominal) sits at the boundary of the anger registry (reg 4) and the abomination/indignation registry (reg 1). H2194 verbal form is in reg 1; H2195 noun form is here. Full treatment requires cross-registry comparison. Current analysis treats H2195 as belonging to anger; reg 1 analysis must address the verbal form. Phase 2 to determine primary ownership.

operations.description: Phase 2 flag: wrath of God eschatological development

record.description: NT orgē as eschatological divine wrath (Rev 6:16-17 wrath of the Lamb; Rev 14:10; 16:1; 19:15) represents a significant theological development of OT divine anger vocabulary. Full treatment requires cross-registry analysis with justice, judgment, and atonement vocabulary. Connection to Isa 53 soul registry (soul poured out) worth exploring: the cross as simultaneous expression of divine anger and divine love.

G3709 orgē has 272 occurrences but only 33 verses in the export. The full Pauline wrath theology and the Revelation bowl judgment sequence require additional verse coverage before synthesis conclusions are drawn. Priority: complete Pauline wrath vocabulary (Romans 1-5, 9, 12, 13; Eph 2, 4, 5; Col 3; 1Th 1, 2, 5) and Revelation wrath sequence (Rev 6, 11, 14, 15, 16, 19).

H7110B (qe.tseph: splinter, Joel 1:7) carries a verse count of 28 erroneously shared with H7110A (qe.tseph: wrath). H7110B is a homonym with no anger content. Assigned delete status in MTI. H7110A holds the correct verse data for the wrath meaning.

G3948 paroxusmos appears both negatively (Act 15:39, Paul-Barnabas sharp dispute) and positively (Heb 10:24, stir up one another to love and good deeds). The stirring-up mechanism that produces relational conflict is also available for positive relational catalysis. Minor but analytically interesting for the relational vocabulary study in Session D.
